title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A gorgeous fable about following your dreams, listening to your heart, and reading the omens strewn along life's path.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: An easy and proven way to build good habits and break bad ones, packed with actionable self-improvement strategies.

title: It Ends with Us
author: Colleen Hoover
description: A brave and heartbreaking novel that digs its claws into you and doesn't let go, exploring love, loss, and resilience.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: The memorable novel of a childhood in a sleepy Southern town and the crisis of conscience that rocked it.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: The classic romantic comedy centered on Elizabeth Bennet and Mr. Darcy as they navigate pride, class, and social standing.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A groundbreaking narrative of humanity's creation and evolution, exploring how biology and history have defined us.

title: Say No to Stress
author: Dr. Vikas Kumar
description: A practical guide outlining effective strategies and lifestyle changes to effectively manage and eliminate daily stress.

title: Surrounded by Idiots
author: Thomas Erikson
description: A revolutionary book that offers simple yet ground-breaking methods for assessing the personalities of people we communicate with.

title: Ugly Love
author: Colleen Hoover
description: When Tate Collins meets airline pilot Miles Archer, they decide on a physical-only relationship, but rules are meant to be broken.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
description: A lone astronaut must save the Earth from an extinction-level event in this gripping, scientifically detailed sci-fi thriller.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
description: A shocking psychological thriller about a woman's act of violence against her husband, and the therapist obsessed with uncovering her motive.

title: Educated
author: Tara Westover
description: An unforgettable memoir about a young girl who leaves her survivalist family in rural Idaho to pursue a PhD from Cambridge University.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
description: Rules for focused success in a distracted world, teaching you how to quickly master complicated cognitive tasks.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A dramatic, stylized portrait of the Jazz Age, exploring themes of wealth, love, ambition, and the American Dream.

title: Circe
author: Madeline Miller
description: An epic retelling of the life of the misunderstood witch-goddess Circe as she carves out her own path in the world of gods and mortals.

author: George Orwell
description: A chilling dystopian masterpiece exploring state surveillance, historical negationism, and totalitarian control over human thought.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: A landmark book that introduces non-specialist readers to the origins, structure, and ultimate fate of the universe.

title: Becoming
author: Michelle Obama
description: An intimate, powerful, and inspiring memoir by the former First Lady of the United States, charting her life and path to the White House.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: A masterly explanation of the two systems that drive our choices: the fast, intuitive system, and the slow, logical one.

title: Steve Jobs
author: Walter Isaacson
description: The definitive biography of the visionary Apple co-founder, based on hundreds of interviews with Jobs, family, and colleagues.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: The enchanting prelude to The Lord of the Rings, following Bilbo Baggins as he is swept into an epic quest to reclaim a lost kingdom.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
description: The magical adventure begins as an ordinary orphan discovers he is a wizard and is invited to attend Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry.

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
description: A brilliant, razor-sharp thriller exploring a toxic marriage and the mysterious disappearance of Amy Dunne on her fifth anniversary.

title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
author: Stieg Larsson
description: A gripping murder mystery, family saga, and financial intrigue featuring the brilliant but deeply troubled hacker Lisbeth Salander.

title: Normal People
author: Sally Rooney
description: An exquisite, tender exploration of the complicated relationship between Connell and Marianne as they grow up together in rural Ireland.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
description: A profound and visionary dystopian work depicting a technologically advanced society that trades freedom and pain for sterile stability.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: A poetic and deep scientific journey into the universe, life, human curiosity, and our place in the cosmic ocean.

title: Guns, Germs, and Steel
author: Jared Diamond
description: A brilliant synthesis of history, geography, and biology explaining why some civilizations succeeded while others fell.

title: Shoe Dog
author: Phil Knight
description: A candid and fascinating memoir by the Nike founder, detailing the early struggles and triumphant rise of an iconic global brand.

title: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
author: C.S. Lewis
description: Four adventurous siblings step through a magical wardrobe and find themselves in the frozen land of Narnia, bound by an icy spell.

title: Big Little Lies
author: Liane Moriarty
description: A thrilling and darkly comic novel about three women, ex-husbands, schoolyard scandals, and a school trivia night that goes fatally wrong.

title: Me Before You
author: Jojo Moyes
description: An incredibly moving love story about a cheerful young woman who takes a job caretaking for a cynical quadriplegic man who wants to end his life.

title: The Book Thief
author: Markus Zusak
description: Set in Nazi Germany, a beautifully written story narrated by Death about a young girl who steals books and shares them with others.

title: Silent Spring
author: Rachel Carson
description: The landmark environmental book that exposed the hazards of chemical pesticides and helped launch the modern ecological movement.

title: The Wright Brothers
author: David McCullough
description: The dramatic story of two brave brothers who taught the world how to fly, capturing the engineering spirit of early 20th century America.

title: Charlotte's Web
author: E.B. White
description: The classic story of a pig named Wilbur and his friendship with an extraordinary, wise spider named Charlotte.

title: Da Vinci Code
author: Dan Brown
description: A pulse-pounding mystery set in Paris, tracking Harvard symbologist Robert Langdon as he decodes symbols hidden in Leonardo da Vinci's art.

title: The Fault in Our Stars
author: John Green
description: A brilliant, funny, and deeply emotional novel about two teenage cancer patients who fall in love and find meaning in their short lives.

title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
description: The foundational science fiction and gothic novel about Victor Frankenstein and the tragic, sentient creature he creates in his laboratory.

title: Origin of Species
author: Charles Darwin
description: The historic, seminal work introducing the scientific theory of evolution by natural selection, changing the course of modern biology.

title: A People's History of the United States
author: Howard Zinn
description: A highly influential work presenting American history through the eyes of the country's marginalized and working-class populations.

title: The Diary of a Young Girl
author: Anne Frank
description: The unforgettable, deeply moving diary kept by a young Jewish girl hiding from the Nazis with her family in occupied Amsterdam.

title: Where the Wild Things Are
author: Maurice Sendak
description: The beautifully illustrated children's story of Max, who sails away to an island inhabited by giant, wild creatures.

title: And Then There Were None
author: Agatha Christie
description: The world's best-selling mystery novel, where ten strangers are lured to an isolated island and murdered one by one according to a nursery rhyme.

title: The Notebook
author: Nicholas Sparks
description: An enduring, emotional story of the lifelong love between Noah Calhoun and Allie Nelson, spanning several decades.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
description: The quintessential novel of teenage rebellion and alienation, narrated by the iconic protagonist Holden Caulfield.

title: Astrophysics for People in a Hurry
author: Neil deGrasse Tyson
description: A quick, witty, and highly readable guide to the basic physics and understanding of our vast cosmos.

title: SPQR: A History of Ancient Rome
author: Mary Beard
description: A refreshing, analytical, and highly detailed exploration of Roman history from its mythical origins to the empire's height.

title: Elon Musk
author: Walter Isaacson
description: An intimate, authorized biography of the controversial and ambitious entrepreneur behind Tesla, SpaceX, and Neuralink.

title: Matilda
author: Roald Dahl
description: The magical tale of an extraordinarily intelligent young girl with telekinetic powers who stands up to her cruel school headmistress.
